original_hypothesis.title: Kernel can be permanently bricked by setting admin/executor to address(0) (no zero-address guard)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Kernel.executeAction allows Actions.ChangeExecutor and Actions.ChangeAdmin to set executor/admin to any address, with no nonzero validation. If set to address(0), the kernel becomes unable to execute privileged lifecycle actions or manage roles, effectively freezing the protocol configuration permanently and potentially preventing recovery from incidents requiring module upgrades or policy changes.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a concrete code defect that can cause irreversible operational DoS if triggered by a privileged operator mistake or compromised executor (LOW realism by threat model).

proof_of_signal.pattern: else if (action_ == Actions.ChangeExecutor) { executor = target_; } else if (action_ == Actions.ChangeAdmin) { admin = target_; } // no target_ != address(0) check

preconditions: executeAction called with Actions.ChangeExecutor or Actions.ChangeAdmin

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Critical governance addresses can be set to unusable values without validation

operational_questions: Are there off-chain operational runbooks preventing zero-address updates?
Is there an emergency recovery plan if kernel is bricked (new kernel migration path)?
validation_requirements: Unit test that sets admin/executor to 0 and demonstrates irrecoverability
Assess whether any policy/module retains independent upgrade control outside kernel (should not)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: ExecuteAction(ChangeAdmin, address(0)) or ChangeExecutor to zero
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Observe inability to grant roles / execute actions

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Cannot patch modules/policies; incident response crippled
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High—may require redeploy/migrate with external coordination

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
